japan.title: Japan
japan.txt:
Japan returns again and again in the work — not the Japan of temples and cherry blossoms, but the one of commuter trains at midnight, rain-slicked alleys, and the peculiar silence of a city of fourteen million.

Shot over three visits between 2022 and 2024, mostly on the JR Chuo line and in the back streets of Shinjuku, Kōenji and Golden Gai. Clément Hirson is an amateur photographer based in Paris. The series is unfinished.

seo.body: Sixteen frames from three trips to Japan between 2022 and 2024. Shot in Tokyo’s Golden Gai, on JR platforms, in Nara’s backstreets and along Kyoto’s temple districts. This series documents the friction between stillness and movement in Japanese urban life — the waiting, the ritual, the anonymous crowd. Black and white documentary photography, available in signed limited editions.

lanzarote.title: Lanzarote
lanzarote.loc: Timanfaya · Famara · El Golfo · Playa Blanca
lanzarote.txt:
Lanzarote is a place of recent memory — César Manrique's white walls, the black lava fields of Timanfaya, the Atlantic mist rolling over Famara. A landscape that looks designed rather than grown.

Shot in January 2023, mostly at the edges of the day when the light goes sideways across the stone and turns everything into shadow and silhouette. Clément Hirson is an amateur photographer based in Paris.

seo.body: Eight photographs from Lanzarote’s volcanic interior and Atlantic coastline, made in winter 2023. The Canary Island’s raw landscape — lava fields, salt flats, wind-beaten palms — becomes a study in contrast and isolation. Documentary photography at the edge of Europe, where agriculture survives on ash and fishermen still work the black-sand shores.

elsewhere.title: Elsewhere
elsewhere.txt:
Elsewhere is the catch-all — everything that doesn't fit Japan or Lanzarote. New York at night, Oslo in winter, the wild Atlantic island of Hoëdic, a street in Hackney, a lake in Lithuania.

What holds them together is not geography but a quality of light: the kind that arrives sideways and leaves quickly, and which requires you to already be standing in the right place. Clément Hirson is an amateur photographer based in Paris.

seo.body: An ongoing series of thirteen photographs made across five countries — street scenes, quiet portraits, and landscapes gathered between assignments. From a basketball court in Harlem to a frozen jetty in Oslo, from a Breton island at low tide to a frost-covered window in Vilnius. Documentary photography as a continuous, unscripted journal.
